basics.name: Niklas Schweiger
basics.label: M.Sc. Student in Robotics, Cognition, Intelligence
basics.summary: Master's student at TU Munich specializing in machine learning, generative models, and inference-time alignment. Passionate about reward-agnostic approaches to steer diffusion and flow models. Former exchange student at Chalmers University of Technology, Sweden.

work.name: Siemens AG
work.summary: Developed a Feature Matching system that compares 3D technical drawings against a manufacturing parts database. Computed distances between CNN embeddings from pretrained models using multi-view renderings of 3D objects to retrieve the closest matching part.
highlights: Built multi-view CNN embedding pipeline for 3D part similarity retrieval
Used pretrained models and embedding distance metrics

work.name: Freisinger Hof
work.summary: Service and kitchen support in the restaurant industry.

courses: Exchange semester at Chalmers University of Technology, Gothenburg, Sweden
Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (from 5th semester)

awards.title: 2nd Place – TUM.ai Makeathon (€3,000 prize)
awards.summary: Built 'Caire', an app designed to overcome language barriers between nurses and patients. Using AI, it extracts medically relevant information from natural speech and automatically generates structured medical reports.

publications.name: Trust-Region Noise Search for Black-Box Alignment of Diffusion and Flow Models
publications.summary: We propose a simple trust-region based search algorithm (TRS) which treats the pre-trained generative and reward models as a black-box and only optimizes the source noise. Evaluated across text-to-image, molecule, and protein design tasks. ICLR 2026, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.
